_meta.notes: Second town SE, between Furrowmouth and Threadwarden. The rope-makers' town. The Furrow widens into a long shallow basin here; flax for rope-fiber grows along the banks. Knot's Landing supplies most of the rigging used by the halfling navy via long barge-trains down to Caelum and out to the Coral Anchorage. The town is named for a single iconic knot the rope-masters can tie blindfolded.

tagline: Where the rope is made. Watch your fingers.
ambientPool: Long ropewalk pavilions stretch parallel to the river; the rope-makers walk backward as they work.
A halfling Drowncaller has come up from the coast to inspect a shipment in person.
Flax is laid out on the bank to ret in the slow current.
A child practices the town's namesake knot on a piece of scrap, again and again.

rooms.summary: A long open-sided pavilion, two hundred yards long, with rope-makers walking the length backwards as the rope twists between them. The structure is the most distinctive in the town.
ambientPool: A rope-maker walks the full length of the walk without looking down — they have done this since they were twelve.
Two strands meet and are spliced; the splicer hums a tune that has only one repeating bar.
An apprentice ties the namesake knot for the eleventh time today and is corrected, gently.
The wind down the walk is steady; it helps the rope lay even.

exits.narrative: Out to the riverbank where the flax retting beds are.
To the coil yard where finished rope is stacked for shipment.

rooms.summary: Riverbank where flax is laid out to ret — soaked in slow water until the fiber loosens from the stem. Smells faintly fermented; the rope-makers find it comforting.
ambientPool: A worker checks a bed of retting flax by feel, satisfied with the give.
An older rope-maker sits on a stump watching the river — they have worked the walk for forty years; they are taking a longer break than usual.
Wading birds pick at insects along the bank.
The river moves just fast enough to do the work and no faster.

rooms.summary: Open yard at the river end of the ropewalk. Finished rope is coiled and stacked, awaiting barge-pickup. The halfling Drowncaller inspects every coil personally before signing the manifest.
ambientPool: A halfling Drowncaller flexes a coil to feel the lay; they nod and move to the next.
A coiler ties off a finished length with the namesake knot — the master will check it later.
Two barge-masters wait by their boats, smoking quietly, watching the inspection without comment.
A heavy stamp comes down on a finished bill: SHIPPED.
